reasons: Kaja can suppress Kagura and prevent her from relying on umbrella movement or cleanse timing after being caught.
Suppression is valuable because Kagura normally survives through debuff removal, spacing, and repositioning.

proof.summary: Kagura wants to escape or cleanse with umbrella mobility, while Kaja can force a suppression pickoff window that prevents the normal reset.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits for Kagura to step forward for umbrella poke.
failureCases: Kagura keeps distance and never enters Kaja range.

reasons: Franco is a stronger reviewed replacement for Saber because suppression is harder for Kagura to answer than normal airborne burst.
If Franco catches Kagura after she steps forward, he can lock her before she repositions with umbrella tools.

proof.summary: Kagura's second skill helps her escape many control windows, but Franco's suppression can still force a pickoff when she is caught in range.
worksBestWhen: Franco holds suppression until Kagura has stepped forward or used her movement timing.
Franco has teammate burst ready after the lock.
failureCases: Kagura keeps distance and pokes from outside Franco's threat range.
Franco misses hook or uses suppression without follow-up damage.

reasons: Lolita can reduce Kagura's umbrella poke value in front-to-back fights by protecting her team from ranged projectile pressure.
This helps the team walk through Kagura's zoning during siege or objective setup.

proof.summary: Kagura wants to control space with umbrella poke, while Lolita can reduce projectile pressure and protect the team during approach windows.
worksBestWhen: The team groups behind Lolita during siege or objective setup.
failureCases: Kagura waits out the shield and repositions the umbrella around it.

reasons: Hayabusa can threaten Kagura from angles that make umbrella zoning harder to maintain.
Kagura is punishable when her umbrella is away or her escape timing has already been used.

proof.summary: Kagura wants controlled spacing, while Hayabusa can use shadow movement to bypass expected front-line zoning and punish cooldown windows.
worksBestWhen: Kagura is separated from support or tank peel.
failureCases: Kagura saves Rasho Umbrella Flee for Hayabusa.
Hayabusa's ultimate damage is split by nearby enemies.

reasons: Chou can threaten Kagura with flank displacement if he baits or waits out her cleanse and movement timing.
This matchup is execution-heavy because Kagura can escape bad Chou timing.

proof.summary: Kagura can cleanse and reposition, so Chou must bait that tool or catch her after it is unavailable before committing to the pickoff.
worksBestWhen: Chou flanks and waits for Kagura's movement skill.
Chou has teammate burst ready after the displacement.
failureCases: Kagura cleanses or dodges Chou's setup.
Chou approaches from vision and gets poked down.
